verdict.answer_first: A states the key actionable issue up front: "The biggest concrete bug is the trailing newline." B instead leads with the incorrect claim "doesn't apply here."
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to an immediate replacement, "function isEmail(s) { return /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(s); }", while A asks "If you tell me which..."
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on reliably because it correctly warns that "foo@bar\n" passes and suggests "testing !s.includes('\n')" or using a tighter regex; B's main safety claim is wrong.
